content: The potential energy of a particle of mass 0.1 kg moving along the $X$-axis, is given by $U=5 x(x-4) \mathrm{J}$, where $x$ is in metres. Choose the wrong option.

options.content: The speed of the particle is maximum at $x=2 \mathrm{~m}$
The particle executes simple harmonic motion
The period of oscillation of the particle is $\frac{\pi}{5} \mathrm{~s}$

explanation:
The potential energy $ U $ of a particle with mass 0.1 kg moving along the $ X $-axis is described by:

$ U = 5x(x-4) \, \text{J} $

where $ x $ is the position in meters.

The equation expands to:

$ U = 5x^2 - 20x $

To find the force $ F $, we take the derivative of $ U $ with respect to $ x $:

$ \frac{dU}{dx} = 10x - 20 $

The force is given by the negative derivative of the potential energy:

$ F = -\frac{dU}{dx} = -10x + 20 $

Assuming a shifted position $ x = (X + 2) $, the force becomes:

$ F = -10X $

The force is zero when $ X = 0 $ or equivalently, $ x = 2 \, \text{m} $. Therefore, $ x = 2 \, \text{m} $ is the equilibrium or mean position about which the particle performs simple harmonic motion (SHM).

The spring constant $ k $ is 10, leading to a period of oscillation $ T $ calculated as follows:

$ T = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}} = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{0.1}{10}} = \frac{\pi}{5} \, \text{s} $

At the mean position, the kinetic energy of the particle is at a maximum, which is consistent with SHM behavior, where the force acting on the particle varies.

content: One end of a spring of force constant $$k$$ is fixed to a vertical wall and other to a body of mass $$m$$ resting on a smooth horizontal surface. There is another wall at a distance $$x_0$$ from the body. The spring is then compressed by $$2 x_0$$ and released. The time taken to stike the wall is
explanation:
The total time from $$A$$ to $$C$$

$$t_{A C}=t_{A B}+t_{B C}=\frac{T}{4}+t_{B C}$$

where, $$T=$$ Time period of oscillation of spring-mass system. $$t_{B C}$$ can be given by

$$B C=A B \sin \left(\frac{2 \pi}{t}\right) t_{B C}$$

Putting $$\frac{B C}{A B}=\frac{1}{2}$$, we get

$$\begin{aligned} t_B & =\frac{T}{12} \\ t_{A C} & =\frac{2 \pi}{3} \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}} \end{aligned}$$
